fixtures.notes: The constructive decoder strands the tall piece in the baseline order, but a single adjacent swap produces a full one-bin layout.
When the baseline decode already fills the bin exactly, search should preserve it and exit after the first non-improving insert-kick pass.
With one grain-constrained part and one explicit clamp keep-out, the baseline order strands a piece but the first insert-neighbor pass still finds the one-bin layout.
The baseline and all adjacent swaps strand the tall strip, but an insert kick that moves it ahead of the small fillers recovers the full one-bin layout.
With unlimited bins the baseline decode opens a second bin for the tall strip, and an insert kick collapses the same piece set back to a one-bin layout.
This strip-layout case scales up the earlier insert example with four filler rectangles; only moving the full-height strip ahead of the fillers recovers the exact one-bin pack.

This larger strip-layout variant mirrors a small constructive benchmark: the baseline opens a second bin for the full-height strip, and an insert kick collapses the same six-piece set back to one bin.

This hole-aware scenario mirrors a small benchmark-style nesting pattern: if the host piece with the internal cavity is delayed until the end, the constructive baseline opens a second bin, but reordering it first lets the side strip fill the right edge and the small squares drop into the hole.
The 4x4 L-shape must be placed first so the 3x2 rectangle can occupy the interior notch and leave one final 1x1 slot; delaying the host shape forces a second bin.
This convex mixed-shape case uses a tall right triangle plus rectangles; a local jostle should move the triangle ahead of the trailing square so the entire set fits in one bin.
This mixed convex layout reuses the rectangle-hexagon geometry from the NFP corpus and should already be optimal in one bin, so search must preserve the constructive baseline.
This mixed convex preserve case uses both exact triangles from the convex triangle-triangle NFP fixture plus a rectangle and square; search should leave the already-valid one-bin baseline unchanged.

This strip-layout case records a real plateau-then-improve sequence from the current engine: search first finds a partial insert improvement, then spends one non-improving plateau iteration, and only after that later insert step reaches the full one-bin packing.
